description_long: This is a weathered stone monument resembling a traditional gravestone or memorial obelisk, with a rectangular base supporting a tall, upright slab. The front face features an ornate laurel wreath relief encircling an inscription panel, though the text is partially eroded and difficult to read. The stone surface displays signs of age, including discoloration, moss or lichen stains, and surface wear. The back is plain, with vertical streaks of weathering and no visible ornamentation or text. The base is broad and rectangular, with a slightly protruding lower step and some chipping at the corners. The overall appearance suggests a historical or commemorative function, commonly found in cemeteries or parks.
description: A weathered stone memorial obelisk with a laurel wreath relief and inscription on the front, set on a rectangular base. The surface shows signs of age and discoloration.
description_view_0: Front view showing the laurel wreath relief and partially legible inscription on the upright slab.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the obelisk and the layered structure of the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, weathered stone surface with vertical discoloration and no ornamentation.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlighting the thickness of the monument and the stepped base.
attribute_inscription: There is an inscription on the front face within the laurel wreath, in a serif style and likely English, but it is partially eroded and not fully legible.
attribute_ornamentation: A raised laurel wreath relief encircles the inscription panel on the front face, adding classical decorative detail.
attribute_base_design: The base is rectangular with a slightly protruding lower step, constructed from the same stone as the main shaft, and shows signs of chipping and weathering.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is gently arched with a subtle peak at the center, lacking any additional decoration or finial.
